subtaskTitle: Stop using herbicides or pesticides that harm beneficial insects and soil life

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) forbade killing bees and ants (Abu Dawud), recognising that these creatures serve essential roles in Allah's creation.

resolved.note: While the sources include a chapter on the prohibition of killing ants in Sahih Muslim (Book 26, Chapter 36) and a story about a prophet rebuked for burning an ant-hill (Sahih al-Bukhari 536), the specific quote regarding bees and ants attributed to Abu Dawud is not in the excerpts.

subtaskTitle: Protect nesting birds, beneficial insects, and small wildlife on your property

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) told of a man who earned Allah's forgiveness for giving water to a thirsty dog (Bukhari and Muslim), and he rebuked a companion who had taken chicks from a nest, commanding him to return them (Abu Dawud).

resolved.note: The story of the man and the dog is recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 174, 551, and 646. The additional story about chicks and a nest attributed to Abu Dawud is not found in the provided sources.

subtaskTitle: Ensure any pets or animals in your care are properly fed, sheltered, and treated with kindness

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever keeps a pet and does not feed it or water it, Allah will hold him accountable on the Day of Judgement" (Muslim).

resolved.note: The sources describe a woman being punished in Hell for locking up a cat and failing to feed or water it (Sahih al-Bukhari 552, 553, 535; Sahih Muslim 1375).

subtaskTitle: Report any cases of animal cruelty you witness to appropriate authorities

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever among you sees a wrong, let him change it with his hand; if he cannot, then with his tongue; if he cannot, then with his heart — and that is the weakest of faith" (Muslim).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih Muslim 79: 'He who amongst you sees something abominable should modify it with the help of his hand; and if he has not strength enough... with his tongue; ... (or) from his heart, and that is the least of faith.'

subtaskTitle: Teach children the Islamic ethic of rahma (mercy) toward all creatures

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Allah shows mercy to those who show mercy to His creation" (Abu Dawud).

resolved.note: While the specific wording attributed to Abu Dawud is missing, Sahih al-Bukhari 373 states: 'Allah is merciful only to those of His slaves who are merciful (to others).'

subtaskTitle: Plant the tree or plant in your garden, balcony, or a community space

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "There is no Muslim who plants a tree or sows a seed, and then a bird, or a person, or an animal eats from it, except that it is regarded as a charitable gift (sadaqah) for him" (Bukhari and Muslim).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 513 and Sahih Muslim 3764: 'There is none amongst the Muslims who plants a tree or sows seeds, and then a bird, or a person or an animal eats from it, but is regarded as a charitable gift for him.'

subtaskTitle: Register and attend at least one event this season

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said that the most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 41 as 'the best deed... is that which is done regularly.' The parallel narration in Sahih Muslim 1710 includes the phrase 'even if they are small.'

subtaskTitle: Consider committing to a regular volunteer schedule with the organisation

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said the most beloved deeds to Allah are the most consistent, even if small (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Duplicate of claim 7; Bukhari 41 notes the best deeds are those done regularly.

subtaskTitle: Commit to a recurring financial contribution or in-person volunteer schedule

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said the most beloved deeds to Allah are the most consistent, even if small (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Duplicate of claim 7; Bukhari 41 notes the best deeds are those done regularly.

subtaskTitle: Share the project with your network to multiply contributions

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever guides someone to goodness will have a reward like the one who does it" (Muslim).

resolved.note: This specific narration regarding the reward for guiding someone to goodness is not identified in the provided sources.

subtaskTitle: Identify underused land — vacant lots, council land, masjid grounds, or school yards

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever revives dead land, it belongs to them" (Abu Dawud) — this is the principle of ihya al-mawat (reviving dead land), and it applies powerfully to neglected urban spaces.

resolved.note: The principle of 'ihya al-mawat' (reviving dead land) is not found in the provided excerpts.

subtaskTitle: Recruit volunteers and allocate plots or shared responsibilities

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "The believers in their mutual kindness, compassion, and sympathy are just like one body" (Muslim).

resolved.note: A relevant chapter title exists in the Sahih Muslim Table of Contents (Book 32, Chapter 15), but the corresponding hadith text describing believers as 'one body' is missing from the excerpts.

subtaskTitle: List your top 10-15 regular purchases by category (food, clothing, electronics, household)

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Each of you is a shepherd, and each of you is responsible for his flock" (Bukhari & Muslim).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 592: 'Everyone of you is a guardian [shepherd], and responsible for what is in his custody [flock].'

subtaskTitle: Research the farming practices behind your regular halal meat supplier

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Allah has prescribed ihsan (excellence) in all things.

resolved.note: While a chapter on 'Good attitude even in slaughter' appears in the Sahih Muslim Table of Contents (Book 25, Chapter 11), the specific hadith text regarding Allah prescribing 'ihsan' in all things is not in the excerpts.

subtaskTitle: Audit your wardrobe — identify items rarely worn and donate them responsibly

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "The son of Adam has no better right than that he would have a house wherein he may live, a piece of cloth whereby he may hide his nakedness, and a piece of bread and some water" (Tirmidhi).

resolved.note: The specific claim regarding the rights of the son of Adam attributed to Tirmidhi is not found in the provided sources.

subtaskTitle: Identify three product categories where you can apply the tayyib standard more rigorously

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Take on only as much as you can do of good deeds, for the best of deeds is that which is done consistently, even if it is small" (Ibn Majah).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 41 and 191 as deeds done 'within your capacity' and 'regularly' or 'little.' The parallel narration in Sahih Muslim (1710 and 1711) includes the phrasing 'even if they are small.'

subtaskTitle: Make the switch and compare quality and cost over one month

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Tie your camel, then trust in Allah" (Tirmidhi) — do the practical work of testing, then trust the outcome.

resolved.note: The specific proverb 'Tie your camel, then trust in Allah' attributed to Tirmidhi is not present in the provided source excerpts.

subtaskTitle: Share your findings with friends and family to encourage collective switching

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Whoever guides someone to goodness will have a reward like the one who did it" (Muslim).

resolved.note: While the sources discuss the rewards for setting a good precedent (Sahih Muslim 2219) or guiding others on a road (Sahih al-Bukhari 141), the specific 'reward like the one who did it' phrasing is not identified.

subtaskTitle: Build relationships with farmers — ask about their growing practices

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "The buyer and the seller have the option of cancelling or confirming the deal as long as they have not parted" (Bukhari) — commerce was conducted face-to-face, with trust built through repeated honest interaction.

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 321 (and 296) as: 'The buyer and the seller have the option of cancelling or confirming the deal unless they separate.' Also found in Sahih Muslim 3656.

subtaskTitle: Review your current investments and superannuation for exposure to harmful industries

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Every flesh that has been nourished by haram, the Fire is more deserving of it" (Tirmidhi).

resolved.note: The specific quote regarding flesh nourished by haram and the Fire is not found in the provided source materials.

subtaskTitle: Donate or sell excess clothing responsibly

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself" (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 12: 'None of you will have faith till he wishes for his (Muslim) brother what he likes for himself.' Also in Sahih Muslim 72.

subtaskTitle: Connect with existing circular economy networks, incubators, or Islamic finance providers

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "The believer to another believer is like a building whose different parts enforce each other" (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 626: 'A believer to another believer is like a building whose different parts enforce each other.' Also found in hadith 468.

subtaskTitle: Set up a simple ordering and distribution system (shared spreadsheet, collection point)

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Make things easy and do not make them difficult" (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 219: 'You have been sent to make things easy and not to make them difficult.' Also in Sahih al-Bukhari 69.

subtaskTitle: Share with your masjid, Islamic centre, and community social media channels

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "When a person dies, their deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who prays for them" (Muslim).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih Muslim 4005 (Book 13), which lists the three ongoing deeds as 'recurring charity, or knowledge (by which people) benefit, or a pious son, who prays for him.'
